headline: Sandra Guazzotti, Amcham: "We see a positive look at Chile and a great commitment of American companies to the country"
text:
This summer ended the two-year term in the presidency of the Chilean-American Chamber of Commerce (Amcham) Sandra Guazzotti, a period in which she had to deal with the social and health crisis  In this context, the entity now-former owner had to explain to U S  investors the scope of these issues, especially the October 18 crisis and the constitutional process that began to live  Oracle executive also takes stock of this interview with her presidency and tells the vision of U S  companies about the country 
How were these years in the business relationship between Chile and the U S  and the role of the camera, at the general level?
- It was two years of constant change, my management began in early 2019, with an Amcham serving 100 years  with the milestone also of having turned 15 years after the signing of the Chile-U S  Free Trade Agreement, which is very fruitful for both countries  In a context where, we as a camera decide that we obviously wanted to work with our partners to project this 21st century Amcham  within a few months, we first experienced the social outburst, then the pandemic, so it was two years of many changes, where we had to change the way we created value to society, to our partners through this full integration with the U S  where we had to put at the center a different look at the role of the company, very in line with what has been living in the USA  
Oracle Senior Vice President for the Amcham Region and President: "We are confident that the principles and values that have differentiated Chile will be projected into the future"The general manager of the local Microsoft subsidiary assumes as new president of AmCham
This role of the company as an aggregator of social value in addition to the economic value and relationship with the USA  These two years have been very fruitful, as it has been during all these years with the FTA, which goes beyond the economic aspect, it is important to highlight it, the relationship between Chile and the USA is partnership, sharing best practices, knowledge, environmental care, sustainability and quality employment generation  Of the US investment we have been the most important foreign direct in Chile, we have a cumulative inventory of about 40 billion dollars  In 2020 we regained first place, with investments in Chile close to $5 billion 
Do China role with its increase in investment in the country see a challenge?
- The U S  is the largest foreign investor in Chile, it has historically been and I re-enter that place in 2020, so we're ahead  in fact, in foreign direct investment, the second country was Canada, and only then comes China  U S  investment increased by 61% vs 2019, we believe this shows the commitment of U S  companies to the future of chili pepper and its long-term look  There is also to note that of the 229 projects prioritized by the government to support the agility of processing for the next two years, more than half are projects of American origin, so, we are seeing from the US an optimistic view of Chile, of the constituent process as an institutional body, to capture the Chile of the future, a process in which it will be very important that the principles and values that have made them also project Chile is an attractive country to invest, so with recent announcements of a lot of investment in areas like technology, we were talking about the opening of Oracle regional cloud, but also the announcements of new Microsoft data centers andother technologies that are seeing in Chile the possibility of addressing the market in the region, a true digital hub, but also in clean energies  We see a positive look at Chile and a great commitment of American companies to the country 
What are the expectations with the new U S  government?
I highlight the democracy and strengths of institutions in what has happened in the U S , which ended President Biden oath in an orderly manner, with a composing message  He arrives with a message of unity and optimism, of building better and being the president of all  In this sense, we have an optimistic look, the signs that have been given in these weeks since he assumed his role as president, with the return to the Paris agreement, the commitment to climate change, the return to multilateral organizations are already positive  Let look at a U S  taking a more leadership role in the international context  We're going to serve 200 years of relations between Chile and the U S  we are well optimistic and more energy-efficient than ever to continue working on the integration between Chile and the US, which not only translates into investment towards Chile, but also the US is very important for Chilean business and entrepreneurship, which wants to scale globally 
What was the most complicated time in these two years?
-In terms of the trade relationship it has been very positive over the years  So perhaps at the presidency level the most challenging thing was in the social outburst to be able to fully explain what was happening in Chile  First of all we understand it, the process of thinking that the moment we are living in is extremely important and that it is not isolated globally and above all the institutional exit that Chile achieves in November 2019 was a very important moment  I traveled several times to the U S  to be able to explain this situation and we always had a lot of support on the side of American companies, but it was a particularly complex time, not just for me, for everyone 
Despite the social crisis, is the confidence of foreign companies maintained?
- First, that the constituent process has been reached in an institutional and orderly way is seen in a very positive way, more so, we are optimistic and see that it can be embodied in this Chile of the 21st century  But it is very important that this is done with respect to commitments made, to international treaties, with all this framework that was already established in November that will have this new constitution  We see it with optimism, we consider it important that the principles and values that have made Chile an attractive country to invest are projected into this new constitution and again we see it as an opportunity and we will be becoming an active part of the talks, with the gaze of foreign investors 
I would also say that the signals are relevant, and where if there is more concern is in some legislative initiatives, given that there will be bills that have to move forward, we cannot wait to resolve emergency issues for Chile, such as the issue of pensions, that a constituent process will be completed, and in this discussion if all investors are looking at the signs , and we all advocate that there be an in-depth discussion, that you look at the short- and long-term effects, this is very important 
In the AFP system there are important U S  investors, how do you see those doubts?
- For us the issue of pensions is very relevant for several reasons, first because it is the future and well-being of our employees and society, it is important for all our partner companies and in Amcham it is one ofthe issues we take very importantly  Also because there is a lot of foreign investment, and in a context where there is a shortage of capital globally, where today, for the economic revival of foreign investment, what happens in the area of pensions is going to be observed and its signals will be important  It is also important for the capital market, we know that the capital market in Chile has been very important for the growth that the country has had  From these three areas we are following the issue of pensions very closely and always contributing with this purposeful look in the sense that there is a background discussion with a long-term look and that it is addressed with resurd 
What remains to be left to move forward with the next administration of Amcham?
- Advance together in the integral, sustained and inclusive development of Chile and what role the company has in this  With a different look at the company, with building, together with all stakeholders shared and sustainable value, then continuing to move forward in that line is essential  Our focus for 2021 will be on the way to continuing to contribute to, first, overcoming the pandemic as soon as possible, which has direct connection not only to the well-being of all as a citizenry, but also to the economic revival and recovery of jobs  So, in that context, continue to boost U S  investment in Chile, but also Chilean entrepreneurship towards the American market, bringing this market closer to Chile  It going to remain a very important focus for us  Continuing to promote, from companies, this look at the role of companies with purpose, with best practices, with good corporate governance, is another front that we will be working on, and of course, we will be very active in the constituent process with an eye on the foreign investor  I anticipate a 2021 of a lot of work and continuity in what we had been doing, I believe that this whole process of transformation over the last two years, having been able to go to a digital format but that contrary to what we thought at the beginning, has allowed us to connect much more with our partners  We multiply by 6 the arrival of our partners, we reach 6 thousand member people during 2020, it is something that we will continue to promote this year 
How do you expect to participate in the constituent process?
- We will participate in different artboards, we have done workshops on what the process is  There is a responsibility we feel from Amcham very strong to share experiences, good practices, what we have learned in the U S  within the context of what is going to be discussed and fundamentally with a lot of focus on issues that are relevant, so that Chile remains an attractive country for foreign investment, which will be key to economic revival  We are working in collaboration with 8 binational cameras to be able to represent the voice of the foreign investor, so we want to be protagonists 
How do Chilean entrepreneurs see this look at the role of companies beyond profits, are open to this?
- I think so, the theme is installed in Chile  The social outburst was a remease at the institutional level, including businesses  Which meant in terms of trust towards institutions and the message to entrepreneur  It was very important this need to connect and have a look at the role of different companies, there has been a lot of reflection, I have participated in many instances, I think that the theme is installed at all level, included in companies and chileans as well  This was evident with the pandemic, the latest IPSOS survey indicates how there has been an improvement inperception of companies, how they have worked contributing to the safety of their employees, but also to the community, with donations, with concrete commitments  I think the pandemic has been harsh, but it has been an instance where Chilean companies have demonstrated this ability to articulate not only economic value, but also an important role in the social fabric  Obviously this is something that has to continue to evolve, and that why from Amcham we think it a value shared with the U S  From Amcham we share best practices on these issues, which translate into issues such as corporate governance, diversity, inclusion and equity, transparency  We have been doing mentoring programs in diversity and inclusion for 3 consecutive years, where companies with more experience mentor those who are starting with these programs in firms, and that are part of this vision of the company, which has to do with this aspect of the ESG, what is the company committed to all stakeholders and commitment in all that is environment , society and corporate governance

headline: Science ministry seeks to inject 16 tln won in 5 yrs to foster AI sector: sources
text:
18 Jun 2025 (Yonhap News Agency) South Korea science ministry has requested a budget of more than 16 trillion won (US$11 7 billion) over the next five years to build national artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure and support AI-related research and development (R and D) activities, industry sources said Wednesday 
The Ministry of Science and ICT has filed the proposal to allocate a total of 16 08 trillion won with a goal of making South Korea one of the world top three AI powerhouses by 2030, according to the sources 
The ministry declined to confirm 
Of the proposed funding, 12 3 trillion won would be earmarked for establishing an "AI Highway," which includes building an AI data center equipped with 50,000 graphic processing units (GPUs), they said 
Additional funding would support AI-related R and D, the development of AI-specialized industrial complexes and policy loan programs  The budget would also be used to nurture skilled professionals to lead innovation in the AI sector 
Separately, the ministry has called for 3 09 trillion won in funding over five years to enhance the country digital infrastructure and improve user convenience 
Of that amount, 2 34 trillion won would be allocated to strengthening cybersecurity and building a safer digital environment 
The ministry also proposed easing the public telecom burden by allowing mobile phone bills to qualify for tax deductions

headline: Sec. Burgum: The future of American energy and mining dominance lies out West
text:
Ellen Jaskol
Interior Secretary Doug Burgum speaks during a meeting of the Western Governor Association in Santa Fe, N M , on Monday, June 23, 2025 
1
SANTA FE, N M  - Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said Monday the West must spearhead solutions to the "existential threat" facing the nation  
It not climate change, which will be solved "in the next decade or two by the innovation that will be provided," Burgum said at the annual meeting of the Western Governors Association 
It supplying the energy and critical minerals necessary to make those innovations possible, he said  
As artificial intelligence strains energy grids and trade wars expose the country reliance on China for rare earth materials, Utah and its neighbors appear the best positioned to fill the gap, according to Burgum  
"Where are most of the critical minerals in this country, and the rare earth minerals, most of them are in the West," Burgum said  "The West remains a complete powerhouse of innovation, productivity and possibility " 
The second Trump administration has issued several executive orders to speed up and expand nuclear energy production and critical mineral extraction in the U S  
But it will take a region experienced with innovating its way out of scarcity to make this "abundance" agenda a reality, Burgum said  
Utah leads on nuclear, critical minerals
This message is not new to Utah, according to Gov  Spencer Cox, who was also at the meeting in Sante Fe, New Mexico, along with governors from Colorado, North Dakota, South Dakota, New Mexico and Wyoming  
Ellen Jaskol
1
In 2023, Utah was ranked as the No  1 place in the world by the Fraser Institute for mining investment because of its regulatory approach and natural resources  
The most recent Utah geological survey shows the state possesses 40 out of the 50 critical minerals considered essential for the national economy and defense  
"Utah has a much more diverse mineral endowment than even most other western states," said Brian Somers, the president of the Utah Mining Association 
Over the past few years, the state has also done more than any other to create a policy environment for nuclear energy, according to Christine Czizmadia, the senior director of state government affairs at the Nuclear Energy Institute  
"Utah out of all those states is doing the most," Czizmadia said  "Cox is trying to create an ecosystem for nuclear in Utah; no other state has done as much for nuclear this fast " 
This includes entering into a partnership with Idaho and Wyoming to collaborate on nuclear energy opportunities, creating a nuclear energy consortium to streamline nuclear policy and investing $8 million in site identification for reactors  
Ellen Jaskol
1
The Beehive State is "absolutely" at the forefront of the Trump administration push to increase energy and mining supply, "and it always has been," Cox said  
"We know we have more critical minerals than any other state," Cox said  "And of course, on the nuclear energy side, Utah is the leader right now in the country "
Is energy dominance cleaner?
Utah potential in energy and mining does not have to come at the expense of environmental or community concerns, Burgum suggested  
Trump "made in America" vision will actually result in a cleaner and cheaper country, he said  
New Mexico Gov  Michelle Lujan Grisham, a Democrat, agreed with the Republican Cabinet secretary that the best thing the West could do for the environment - and its economy - would be to produce more energy, including with fossil fuels  
"If we were using the Western states' (liquefied natural gas) around the world, we can reduce those carbon footprints by 40% and we're ready to do it now," Grisham said  
Kristin Murphy, Deseret News
Natural Gas DNSTOCK_KM_05 JPG
1
Burgum, a former tech executive and two-term governor of North Dakota, said that states can no longer think in terms of an energy transition, away from fossil fuels like coal and toward renewables like wind and solar  
They have to think in terms of "energy addition " 
Burgum told the room of lobbyists, journalists and industry experts that this position has nothing to do with ideology or climate change  
"It just about physics," Burgum said  
AI data centers demand so much power that states cannot rely on "intermittent" energy sources that stop contributing to the grid when the sun goes down or the wind stops  
Jeffrey D  Allred, Deseret News
Meta Data Center Tour_ja_0709 jpg
1
And the states that get it right will become "magnets for capital" because today automized factories take more energy than ever before, he said  
"If you're going to re-shore your manufacturing back in the United States, you're not going to go to a state that got 30 or 40% higher electric costs and protest every time someone tries to build an LNG pipeline," Burgum said  
Recently Utah has received interest from multiple AI data centers and "every nuclear company in the world," according to Cox  
The governor said the Trump administration focus on innovation, and its focus on states like Utah in the West, is a great sign for what to come - and a recognition of where Utah has been heading for years  
"We've been preaching this for years, now, finally the rest of the country is starting to listen," Cox said

headline: Senate passes bill giving tax breaks to eligible data centers locating in Connecticut
text:
Mar  2-The state Senate on Monday evening passed a bill that would provide data centers locating in Connecticut with tax breaks if they invest a certain amount of money, a measure that has garnered enthusiastic support from some eastern Connecticut legislators and advocates for the building trades 
The Senate passed the bill 29-5, after the House passed the bill by a 133-13 vote last Wednesday, which Gov  Ned Lamont applauded 
H B  6514 An Act Concerning Incentives for Qualified Data Centers to Locate in the State defines a qualified data center as a facility that houses networked computer servers in one location, thus centralizing the storage and dissemination of data 
The legislation allows exemptions from sales and use, property, and financial transactions taxes, if the developer of the data center applies to the commissioner of the Department of Economic and Community Development and meets certain requirements within five years 
To get a 20-year agreement, the data center must make an investment of $50 million if the center is located in an enterprise zone or opportunity zone, or $200 million if it isn't  The commissioner can extend the agreement to 30 years if the investment is $200 million in an enterprise zone or opportunity zone, or $400 million otherwise 
A developer or owner can't begin construction or renovation of a data center until entering into an agreement negotiated with the host municipality, which could require payments in lieu of taxes  If the terms of the agreement aren't met, the data center has 180 days to remedy the issue or else the agreement can be terminated and the data center is liable for taxes 
The state nonpartisan Office of Fiscal Analysis doesn't anticipate any revenue impact from the bill  Data centers would be required to pay up to $50,000 annually to a new office within DECD, but OFA anticipates that would cover additional administrative costs 
Randy Collins from the Connecticut Conference of Municipalities said in a public hearing last week he hearing that these types of facilities are being considered for eastern Connecticut, and he had conversations with Groton, Montville and Norwich 
Collins stressed repeatedly that towns don't have to take advantage of this opportunity, that they can say no to an agreement they don't find beneficial 
Some legislators expressed that they think a 30-year period is too long for a tax abatement program, but Sen  Heather Somers, R-Groton, said without 30-year agreements, data centers likely won't come 
A vocal advocate for the bill, Somers said it would create a landscape needed to increase jobs, decrease reliance on the gaming and defense industries, and provide opportunities for land that otherwise isn't being used 
She stressed that hyperscale data centers need consistent energy, fiber connectivity, and land, but unlike other buildings that require sewer and other infrastructure, data centers could be located in more rural areas  They also are attacted by tax breaks 
Somers also noted that data is doubling every 18 months and there not enough cloud space for storage, and that Connecticut hospitals have their storage in Indiana 
"Connecticut is well positioned to attract investment from this growing industry that seeks a highly skilled workforce and benefits from proximity to the state established industries: finance, technology, defense, health care, and insurance," Chamber of Commerce of Eastern Connecticut President and CEO Tony Sheridan said in written testimony supporting the bill  Sen  Cathy Osten, D-Sprague, also submitted written testimony in favor 
Sen  Rob Sampson, R-Wolcott, one of the five to vote against the bill, slammed it as corporate welfare, saying the state shouldn't "give any more power to a few well-connected people to decide who wins and who loses " Sen  Matt Lesser, D-Middletown, who also voted against the bill, tweeted that tax competition with neighbors is "bad, self-defeating policy "
In response to Sampson, Sen  Norm Needleman, D-Essex, said Connecticut is competing with 49 other states, and that a difference between prior incentives is the state isn't giving anything upfront 
Needleman said in addition to the "huge amount of money required to construct one of these facilities, the ever-hanging technology means much of the equipment becomes obsolete within 2-3 years and has to be replaced "
DECD Commissioner David Lehman said some of these centers can have 1,000 or more jobs needed to construct them over 18 or 24 months  Joe Toner of the Connecticut State Building Trades Council said there is 30% unemployment in the trades and many members are traveling to New York, Massachusetts, and Rhode Island, making this bill a great opportunity  Osten estimated a data center would generate about 50 permanent jobs and 1,500 construction jobs 
Beyond job creation, Lehman said the two main benefits are growth in a town grand list and income tax revenue  He also commented, "When you have state-of-the art data centers, you generally are going to have financial and technology jobs that want to be proximate to those data centers "
He said this legislation would not impact existing data centers unless they decided to do a large enough renovation or new construction 
Monday morning, Senate Democratic leadership said in a press release they expected the bill to pass, but announced a commitment to vote on another bill later in the session "to address the environmental impacts of data centers, including green building standards, emission standards for diesel generators used by data centers, and environmental justice "
Paul Mounds, chief of staff to the governor, said in a statement Monday the Lamont administration is committed to passing an environmental bill later this session that augments the current legislation 
This was a concern that Sen  Christine Cohen, D-Guilford, chairwoman of the Environment Committee, expressed last week and on Monday, noting that data centers use "massive amounts of electricity "
e moser@theday com

headline: Sergio Rademacher, Amcham: "Changes in the rules of the game have to be approached very carefully and accompanied by a technical debate of the impact"
text:
The new president of the Chilean American Chamber of Commerce (Amcham) began his administration this summer in the midst of the health crisis and the beginning of the constituent process, that is, it is a key moment to analyze the evolution of investment in the country 
Sergio Rademacher, who is also the general manager of Microsoft Chile, has in his position Amcham to make the voice of his partner companies feel and also, he indicates, collaborate in technical aspects and best practices in the development of local companies and the economy  Thus, one of his first actions as owner of the guild was to meet in February with the, at that time also newcomer, Minister of Finance, Rodrigo Cerda 
How was the meeting with the minister of finance?
It was a good time to share, to also introduce myself to this new role as president of Amcham and also to tell him some issues that are of interest to the partners  It was a good meeting and a good reception too 
Did any particular concerns have been brought to you by American companies?
It was more protocol  Now, the issues that concern partners are well-known topics  Obviously the debate that we are seeing today, because while we value very much the path that Chile has chosen to face the challenges, it is tremendously important that the differences are resolved by an institutional process, always promoting dialogue, always propositivo  That the best way to contribute to the country development 
How are the business relations between Chile and the U S  today?
They're good  It a good time  It is interesting how Biden is stressing the importance of multilateral relations and I believe that his signals have been quite clear in international matters  There is an intention to regain the active position that the U S  had for a long time at the multilateral level 
What expectations do you have with the Biden administration in relation to the previous one?
President Biden is not someone far from Latin America  In the Obama administration it was very close to the region  And we as a camera have always played a very important role in promoting integration between the two countries and we will continue to do so  It is tremendously important how we open this space for public-private dialogue, especially by taking advantage of the exchange of good practices  The experience and leadership of U S  companies on issues as important as corporate governance, sustainability, diversity and inclusion, and digital transfusion as well  It a very good example for your Chilean peers, it important to get that conversation back  I am sure that this relationship will continue to grow and will continue to have a positive impact, with a fed-up focus on economic growth and recovery 
Is the U S  losing relevance in the region in relation to China push?
Before we talk about China, I think it important to highlight the difference or one of the big assets that U S  investment has  The U S  is by far the largest foreign investor in Chile, with more than $40 billion accumulated investments, and this is a relationship that has been very long-standing  If we look for example in the case of Amcham, which we have always been promoting exchange between countries, it is more than 100 years old, therefore it is a very long-term relationship and very deep and important  Another very important issue is that today American investment in Chile, more than just a financial investment, seeks growth in the country and American companies have made a super important contribution in generating value jobs, developing human capital and also technologies that improve people lives  Good practices in corporate governance, in transparency, in R&D  In the end, it is a very rich investment, which goes beyond an amount, but how we manage to make this sustainable over time and generate a long-term contribution in the country 
What about the Chinese onslaught?
Chile has always been a well-opened economy and its strategy has always been to open new and larger markets  This has allowed it to access a market of more than 5 billion people today, so I think it is important that Chile continues to develop treaties with different countries to obviously increase its access to other markets 
But is there no concern about this increased competition from the Asian country?
Competition is always a positive thing, it always ends up benefiting, so we don't have any problems with that and neither does a concern  The U S  relationship with Chile, or the relationship of companies that have historically invested in Chile is very long-standing, so we don't see competition or a greater challenge with that  We think we're going to keep making the investments  For U S  companies Chile remains a very interesting country to be able to invest and they will continue to develop their long-term plans 
How do you see the business prospects going forward with the health crisis?
Separating the different issues, I think the issue of the pandemic is something that has affected the whole of society and the impact it will have is going to be lasting  Now, at Amcham we have an optimistic vision and believe that we can contribute very actively to the revival of the country  Even within the context of Covid, foreign investment, particularly from the U S  as the main investor, has remained active  That hasn't stopped and we've seen several announcements from some companies, for example, the first data center that Oracle opened a while ago  We ourselves at Microsoft announce the first Data Centers region in Chile, with the different fronts that that have  Today collaboration with Pfizer and Johnson & Johnson, with vaccines and their close relationship with the government, which has enabled Chile, among other things, to take global leadership in the inoculation process  We have AES Gener who has made important announcements in decarbonization, hand in hand with the Ministry of Energy to help change the energy matrix that we have in Chile or, finally, tremendously innovative things such as the thermal plant of Cerro Dominador, which will allow a supply of 24 hours  Therefore, I would say that at the macro level we continue to see Chile with good eyes, precisely because of the great record track it has 
And in view of the constituent process?
The truth is that the constituent process is a great opportunity for Chile to re-confess its commitment to democratic institutionality, which is a principle of high importance to the investor  Certainly, as in all things, there are risks and uncertainty, that is a natural thing, but in Amcham we believe that through dialogue and collaboration the country will be able to make a leap forward, to a future that is more prosperous and also more supportive  We also believe that the Constitution will incorporate values and principles of the democratic tradition that Chile has had, including its legal certainty, rule of law, independence from powers, commitment to international agreements  For investors the truth is an issue that is tremendously important  And I think it important for us as Chileans to give this confidence and this certainty for the long term 
There are large U S  investors in the AFP industry, and that one of the areas that may have changes to the new Constitution  Are you worried?
On the subject of pensions, and actually in any sector where there is foreign investment, I think it is very important that changes in the rules of the game have to be addressed very carefully and always accompanied by a technical debate of the impact  In the case of the pension debate, I think the focus should be on how we manage an agreement to increase Chileans' pensions, in a process that has to be fairly addressed with a lot of resurability and a long-term view  If you look at the long term, one of the great benefits one could have is how we take that capital and how long-term investment in companies is being encouraged, thanks to access to the capital market, which is one of the factors that has driven development in Chile  To the extent that we have that clear, we will give a good signal to foreign investors, in the sense that, if regulation is changed for the benefit of Chilean society, the rules of the game and the legal certainties it has had continue to be maintained  Therefore, it is tremendously important how we accompany a debate that measures the impact, without losing focus, which isthat people have better old age and a better retirement 
But there is a chance that AFPs, as we know them today, will stop working   
At Amcham we believe that, in every order of things, dialogue is important for democracy  In that sense, we will certainly be watching with great attention how the debate evolves, providing technical information that helps to make the best decisions  That is part of the process, of the entire democratic system, that countries are reviewing how the issue of pensions is precisely configured 
What are the focuses you'll have as president of Amcham?
Enough things  It a very interesting year like Amcham, and the first thing is that we've been working on axes, and a very important issue is how we focus on promoting good business practices and fostering sustained growth  As one takes best practices and implements them, this helps to generate a tremendous positive impact on society  Private activity has to generate progress in the communities it is embedded in  And together with all Amcham member companies that work or collaborate, we always have the same look  Another issue that is going to be quite strong in my management has to do with diversity and inclusion  Continuing to drive female leadership is a tremendously positive thing and is going to be an important issue in economic revival, because we have a tremendous amount of female talent that we need to make sure they return to the labor market 
Do you have any specific goals for your management?
Our main goal is to make our look of making company, where the success of companies goes beyond generating wealth or profitability for shareholders, is something that permees just in Chilean society, and is what we are looking for  This goes for diversity and inclusion, sustainability, environment  That there are no false dichotomies that some organizations have, perhaps with a slightly more traditional look, to say "either I have to be sustainable or I have to be profitable " Successful management would be to have been able to make an impact on that, to have promoted that different way of doing business, that is something shared, that is no exception 
Technology issues have seen large investments in the country in recent times and others have already been announced  Will it follow that trend?
It is very good news for the country and speaks very well of the tradition that Chile has had in terms of how to attract foreign investors to Chile  Many of the companies are Americans, which is precisely this investment that is not only a traditional investment, but also involves developing new capacities, new skills, and also new professions  It a super positive thing  He talks about Chile good history of respecting institutions, how to resolve our differences by talking  How it has been a country that attracts investment, which keeps its promises in the long run  As we continue to do that, it going to continue to attract more investment  In the technology sector, in the health sector, pharmaceuticals, energy  Today it is attracting a lot of investments in the issue of energy and not only to transform the energy matrix that we have, but to bring state-of-the-art technology, which will in the end help the development of Chile  A lot of global investments, when you evaluate where to invest, access to clean energy is very important  Chile has a position to take a lead  We have a tremendous opportunity as a country, we are in a super interesting moment when we are talking about how we want to shape this Chile of the future and incorporate this vision of sustainability, how we make these investments sustainable in the long term, how we manage to incorporate these good practices, how we get inclusion part of organizations

headline: Shaping the future of digital Philippines
text:
27 February 2025 (Philippines News agency) By putting cybersecurity at the forefront, President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr  is laying the groundwork for a safer, stronger digital environment to allow businesses to grow, individuals to communicate and do transactions online with peace of mind, and the government to deliver services more efficiently 
The President took a big leap in strengthening the country cybersecurity by issuing Executive Order (EO) No  58 on April 4, 2024  The order officially adopts the National Cybersecurity Plan (NCSP) 2023-2028, developed by the Department of Information and Communications Technology (DICT)  With this initiative, the administration reaffirms its commitment to a safer and more resilient digital environment for all Filipinos 
For President Marcos, boosting the safety and reliability of Philippine cyberspace is a top priority  This is part of the Philippine Development Plan for 2023-2028  With the increasing reliance on digital platforms across various sectors, this directive is a timely response to emerging cyber threats and vulnerabilities, he said 
Under EO 58, the NCSP 2023-2028 is officially designated as the nation comprehensive roadmap for cybersecurity  The plan provides a clear policy direction and operational framework, ensuring an integrated, whole-of-nation approach to securing the country digital infrastructure 
The order requires all government agencies and local government units (LGUs) to work together  They need to help make the NCSP 2023-2028 a success 
The DICT is tasked with developing an effective system to monitor and evaluate the execution of the plan  In collaboration with the private sector, the DICT will also extend technical assistance to various government agencies to enhance cybersecurity measures across the country 
Government agencies drive digital progress
As the Philippines moves forward on its path to digital transformation, the Executive Branch has been actively pursuing key initiatives under the NCSP 
For example, the Department of Agriculture (DA) is spearheading smart ICT projects to modernize farming in the country  These efforts not only support the government broader goal of strengthening digital infrastructure but also aim to foster data-driven governance and empower local communities, all through the power of technology
On June 10, 2024, DA rolled out several initiatives aimed at bringing technology to the agricultural sector  It created a Digital Agriculture Team under the DA-Philippine Rural Development Project (PRDP)  This team focuses on using technology to improve productivity, sustainability, and resource management in agriculture 
The DA has made it simpler for farmers and fishermen to sign up and get vital government services  They have updated the Registry System for Basic Sectors in Agriculture (RSBSA) to streamline the process and ensure that those in the agricultural sector receive the support they need more efficiently 
The DA also set up the Agriculture-based Central Data Ecosystem (AbCDE), a centralized platform that brings together all agricultural data for smarter decision-making and more effective policy development 
Staying true to its commitment to drive digital transformation and promote open governance, the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) on December 4, 2024 launched the Budget and Treasury Management System (BTMS), that enables real-time online accounting, monitoring, and oversight of obligations and disbursements, providing crucial insights for making informed decisions on managing government resources 
DBM Secretary Amenah Pangandaman said the BTMS will foster a transparent and open government, offering a centralized database that streamlines and organizes all government financial operations  This move reflects a step toward greater accountability and efficiency in public finance 
Said Pangandaman: '[T]he BTMS is more than a system and a technological upgrade - it is a crucial step in turning the principles of transparency, inclusivity, and sustainability into tangible actions, a reflection of our resolve to build a governance structure that better serves the present and future Filipinos "
On Dec  13, 2024, the DBM Procurement Service launched the PhilGEPS  eMarketplace which lets agencies use their devices to procure supplies directly from trusted suppliers 
These initiatives clearly show the Marcos administration dedication to using technology as a cornerstone of national development  As the country moves forward with these transformative ICT projects, it opens up new doors for growth and progress across all sectors, while building a more inclusive, digitally connected society for everyone 
Partnership with South Korea
In a world where technology is constantly evolving, the Philippines is entering an exciting new chapter  The country is forming a partnership that holds the potential to transform its future, backed by South Korea cutting-edge digital expertise 
Called the Korean-Philippine Economic Innovation Partnership Program (EIPP), this collaboration marks a big step forward in the country digital transformation  It a journey that reflects the vision of President Marcos, who sees the power of technology as key to driving progress 
On Jan  16, 2025, leaders from the Philippines and South Korea gathered for the first meeting of the EIPP  This marks a new step in the country tech growth  Led by the National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) and the Korea Trade-Investment Promotion Agency (KOTRA), this partnership focuses on modernizing Philippine digital infrastructure with three game-changing projects 
The first initiative, the National Communications Satellite Roadmap, is all about connecting the Philippines like never before  This plan aims to strengthen the country satellite communications network, ensuring that even the most remote regions are digitally linked  It a bold move that magnifies the vision of President Marcos, who dreams of a nation where technology brings people closer, no matter where they are 
The second project, the Philippine E-Government Master Plan, is all about transforming the way the government interacts with its constituents  It promises to make government services quicker, simpler, and more accessible for everyone  Picture a future where dealing with government processes isn't a headache, where services are easy to navigate and truly responsive to people needs  This is the vision the plan strives to bring to life, building on the Marcos administration commitment to making public services better for all Filipinos 
Finally, the launch of the Integrated Data Center is set to change the way the government handles and secures its data  Backed by Korea top-tier expertise in data management, this project ensures the Philippines is equipped with the modern infrastructure needed to keep pace with the demands of a fast-moving digital economy 
It more than just a technical upgrade-it a step toward building a thriving digital ecosystem that can fuel innovation and growth  In many ways, this initiative reflects President Marcos' vision of a future where technology serves as the backbone of the nation progress 
Joseph Capuno, Undersecretary for Investment Programming at NEDA, shared his excitement about this partnership: 'By working together, we can create a future where our countries do well in the global economy  We'll focus on innovation, teamwork, and making progress together '

headline: Site Selection's economic development rankings put Ohio near the top
text:
Ohio once again is in elite company in an influential ranking of economic development activity 
The state ranked No  3 for total economic development projects, as well as No  3 for projects per capita, in the new Governor Cup rankings from trade publication Site Selection 
Site Selection rankings are based on each state corporate facility projects in 2024 that involved a minimum $1 million investment, creation of 20 or more jobs, or new construction of 20,000 or more square feet 
Site Selection credited Ohio with 565 qualifying projects in 2024, behind only Texas, which had 1,368, and Illinois, which had 664 
There was considerable distance between third-ranked Ohio and the states immediately behind it: No  4 California, with 323 qualifying projects, and Georgia, at 262 
The top five states based on the raw number of projects in 2024 are exactly the same, and in the same order, as they were based on 2023 data, according to Site Selection 
On a per-capita basis, the top state in the 2024 data was South Dakota, which held onto its title from 2023  The rest of the top five on the per-capita list are Illinois, Ohio, Texas, and Kentucky 
JobsOhio, the state private economic development corporation, noted that the 565 projects from 2024 represented about $20 billion in capital investment  Ohio "is the only state to rank in the top five nationally for total project completions and per capita deals closed yearly since 2019," JobsOhio said in a news release highlighting the state performance in the Site Selection rankings 
Gov  Mike DeWine said in a statement that the Site Selection results "highlight our sustained excellence and momentum across every corner of the state " He added, "Ohio is demonstrating that our business-friendly approach, strategic investments in infrastructure, and commitment to workforce readiness are creating an environment where companies and people can thrive "
Some of Ohio highest-profile wins in 2024 included Anduril Industries' plan for a 4,000-employee manufacturing facility in Columbus that will produce tens of thousands of autonomous systems and weapons annually and Amazon Web Services' $10 billion planned data center infrastructure expansion statewide

headline: Skiing and Climate Change: How The World's Environmental Crisis Could Harm Winter's Biggest Sport
text:
When we discuss climate change, we often do so through an economic and sustainability lens  According to a 2018 report by the U N  Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change, the worst impacts arising from climate change could be irreversible by 2030, issues including but not limited to particulate pollution, intense storms, extreme flooding, and disease outbreaks from thawing of ice in the Arctic all have the potential to fundamentally shift the trajectory of life on Earth  
One of the lesser discussed, yet still important, side effects of global warming is the destruction projected to occur to popular winter sports like skiing  Skiing has already seen its fair share of damage - in the United States alone, the average time between the last frost of the spring and the first of the winter season has extended by 10 days since the first half of the 20th century  Many small towns across rural America have begun to feel the effects of shifting weather patterns; for skiing hotspots such as Hayward, Wisconsin, the slopes drive tourism  Hayward is home to the American Birkebeiner, the largest cross-country ski race in North America: however, in 2017, the event was cancelled due to a lack of snow in the area  While this occurrence may seem isolated, the onset of strange weather trends that have been occurring across the country suggest that the snow loss is anything but normal  Just this past year, we saw widespread power outages in Texas due to a massive blizzard that would've been considered impossible a decade ago  As weather patterns gradually shift, it seems inevitable that winter sports like skiing will see a decline  According to Twila Moon, a research scientist with the National Snow and Ice Data Center, we are already seeing massive snow pattern shifts that are severely impacting where and when skiing can be conducted throughout the nation  "Now because of human-caused changes to the climate, we are seeing shorter ski seasons and the quality of our snow is also changing," Moon stated 
The correlation between climate change and skiing becomes even more surprising when observing the effects it has had on low altitude ski resorts throughout Austria  According to Mark Olefs, the head of the climate research department at Vienna Central Institute for Meteorology and Geodynamics, the impact of carbon pollution will disproportionately affect lower-level regions of the world  "If greenhouse gas emissions continue at the same level, snow will almost disappear at lower levels by the end of the century," he warned  Furthermore, many ski resorts have already had to adjust to the changing climate, relying upon artificial snow cannons to keep their trails intact  Although this practice is often successful in the short-term, in the long run snow cannons are predicted to be less and less efficient resources as temperatures rise in lower regions of the world  The Italian Alpines are a prime example of the devastating effects that climate change can have on winter sports, with nearly 200 ski resorts shutting down in the area as the snow season has shrunk by 38 days since 1960  While the fate of skiing remains uncertain in low-altitude communities, there is still hope for the recreational skier  Many communities have taken to the popular solution of constructing new trails at higher elevations, allowing ski fans a chance to return to the slopes once more  As the world pushes forward in the face of climate change, it has become abundantly clear that we need to change our ways to save the environment  The impact of climate change on winter sports allows for a unique perspective on our current economic and environmental crises, highlighting the need for countries to rally behind scientists in order to prevent any further harm to the globe

headline: So real estate investors can take advantage of the data center boom
text:
Demand for data centers breaks records driven by the growing migration to the cloud of IT processes, a business opportunity for real estate investors that offers high returns and rental contracts of ten or more years 
The announced partnership this week between socimi Merlin Properties and US technology company Edged Energy to build four data centers with capacity for 80 megawatts (MW) in the Iberian Peninsula (Madrid, Bilbao, Barcelona and Lisbon) confirms the interest that experts had been advancing 
According to consultancy JLL, although a small number of cabinets providing local support can be considered a CPD (data processing center), the booming asset in the real estate sector are medium or large buildings exclusively dedicated to housing those cabinets full of computer equipment, which are the physical supports of "the cloud" and that manage millions and millions of data 
In Spain, where there were 105 such buildings in 2019, these assets are concentrated in technological parks, with good access to fiber optic and electrical networks 
Among the areas with a greater number of technological parks are that of Alcal'a de Henares in Madrid (where the Tecnoalcal'a Technological Park is located in which the data center of Telef'onica is housed, among others), Cerdanyola del Vall'es in Barcelona (Parc Tecnol'ogico del Vall'es), Paterna in Valencia (Parque Tecnol'ogico de Paterna) and Zaragoza (Waqla Technology Park) 
Paula Albadalejo, JLL Director of Projects and Development Services, says that companies that need the services of a data center are increasingly demanding closeness in their location and that "cities like Valencia and Zaragoza, which are very well connected, will continue to gain momentum in the coming years" 
At CBRE they believe that this segment will continue to grow in 2021 especially in Barcelona and Madrid and that new operators will enter the market 
Experts explain that Spain is called to play an important role within Europe in the data center market since the arrival on the peninsula of three underwater cables connecting it to the United States, Latin America and North Africa 
Amazon Campus in Aragon
The largest project currently under development is the construction in Aragon by Amazon Web Services of a campus of more than 100,000 square meters with three data centers, each with more than 100 megawatts (MW) of power, which is scheduled to open by the end of 2022 or early 2023 
The campus of Amazon and others such as the one developed by Interxion, owned by Digital Realty, in Madrid capital with an area of 35,000 m2 and 34 MW of electricity power, or the one that the Japanese telecommunications giant NTT will open in Las Rozas (Madrid) of 6 MW make Madrid the fastest growing market by absorption in 2020 , with a scale return similar to Paris, according to real estate consultant Knight Frank 
Google and Microsoft have also shown interest in installing data centers in Spain, so Colliers estimates that in the next five years only direct investment in physical data center infrastructures in this country could reach 3 billion euros and that, together, the data center market will receive more than 9 billion euros of investment 
Take-off in Spain comes after the one that started a lustro in other countries in Europe, where the largest markets are London, Frankfurt, Amsterdam and Paris and where the report "Trends in the real estate market in Europe 2021", prepared by PwC and the Urban Land Institute, puts the data centers at the forefront of the 'top ten' of the sectors to follow

headline: So we will live, together, in the future
text:
How are we going to live together? Commissioner Hashim Sarkis has summoned 112 participants from 64 countries to answer this question at the XVII Venice Biennale of Architecture, which opens its doors tomorrow  The issue couldn't be more timely  In a world that barely glimpses the end of the pandemic, marked by climate crisis, mass migration, and racial, social and economic inequalities, traditional models of life are under review and foster a brainstorm, like this one in the Biennial, to respond to the housing challenges of a future that is already here  Sarkis has organized these answers into five chapters, in Arsenal and the Central Pavilion, entitled Between Diverse Beings, The New House, Emerging Communities, Across Borders and A Planet  63 national pavilions complete the Biennial  And, on the outside, 17 collateral events 
Among the contributions to the first chapter are combative ones, such as Y our restroom is a battlefield, by Cassani, Gal'an, Munuera and Sanders, where it is demonstrated by ancient aftertaste teatrillos that a seemingly neutral place like the public washbasin can host very different episodes depending on the US, Haiti or China  There are poetic ones, such as Studio Ossidiana Variations on a Bird Cage, where humans' relationship with birds is explored  And there are ironic ones, such as Tim Parsons and Jessica Charlesworth Catalog for the post human, who wonder: how will we live together if we are forced to increase our competitiveness? His answer is the creation and deployment of a catalog of products, still fictional today, such as a wristwatch that sets the limit of assumed stress, or a vest with nutrient dispenser to work without interruptions 
Hashim Sarkis: "This selection tells us that dialogue must be fostered to learn from each other"
Some of the most powerful proposals are in the chapter The New House or Emerging Communities, also in the Cordeler'ia of Arsenal  In a rejuvenated Biennale, with a strong presence of thirtysomething and aquarenton professionals, the spectacular proposal of the veteran American studio SOM: Life Beyond Earth hits  Its purpose is to explore the possibilities of living in un inhabited environments, with the splendid model of a settlement on a lunar plateau and also with that of a four-storey housing capsule  It also imposes Material Culture, the proposal of Achim Menges and Jan Knippers, a pioneering structure made only of glass and carbon fibers - except the staircase to the first floor - under the pretext that fiber compounds are dominant in the constructions of nature and that they could perhaps be an alternative to conventional construction, which pollutes so much 
Alongside these proposals are others of another scope  Like Fernanda Canales, who in After de house: Privacy in a shared world, explores the recovery of traditional Mexican "neighborhoods" with buildings in which Rossi, Memphis, Escher or Terragni resonate  Or as Ground, the 22x3 meter, lead-colored and gold mosaic of Portuguese Francisco and Manuel Aires Mateus, a delicate and very beautiful reflection on space  Or like Hacking the resort, by Cape Verdeans Anahory and Schofield, who questions the opulence of tourist facilities in a poor environment 
In this section are present the four Spanish guests: Lacol, with its Barcelona block of cooperative housing of La Borda; Benedetta Tagliabue, with her projects for metro station, urban plan and housing in Clichy-Montfermeil (Paris); Rojo/Fern'andez Shaw, with its sober installation of impenetrable rooms; o Practice, with its river regeneration project in Cluj-Napoca (Romania) 
The Central Pavilion houses the proposals of Across borders and A Planet, again diverse  Starting with Obsidiana rain, in which Karanja and Mutegi evoke a grotto, roofed with obsidian stones, in which the anti-colonialist militants met mid-the last century  And following, later, with Recovering the Sublime, a delicious installation of Agapakis, Ginsberg and Tolaas that recovers the fragrance of the Hibiscadelphus Wilderianus, disappeared in 1912 in Hawaii and now recovered with DNA techniques from dry plants: an invitation to reflect on what has let the human being lose, but also an emotional sensory reunion  There are also reflections in the Central Pavilion on what will become of Venice when the temperature has risen 5 degrees per year and also on how the poles merge or, without going so far, the Alps 
Spain with very young eyes
In the first room of the Pavilion of Spain receives the visitor a cloud consisting of more than 400 folios: the requests for participation received in his day by the curators, the thirty-year-old Tenerife Domingo Gonz'alez, Andrzej Gwizdala, Fernando Herrera and Sof'ia Pi~nero  "We are the first commissioners elected by competition; in fact, this commissioner can be seen as our first professional project," they say  Its selection - 34 of the proposals received - is entitled "Uncertainty" and is very heterogeneous, because diverse are also the ways to deal with uncertainty in the architectural profession, and "because we prioritize works with a positive social impact over those where the iconic architectural component prevails"  This selection includes from a new piece of urban furniture, a bank for migrants signed by Baum Arquitectura, to a poetry by the recently deceased architect and poet Joan Margarit, to a renovating residence for elders of Oscar Miguel Ares in Aldeamayor de San Mart'in, or Kinesofos, an audiovisual mechanism of Sergi Hern'andez Carretero that synthesizes and visually captures the movement  "Sarkis says the plurality of voices enriches coexistence," the commissioners recall  "Our proposal is titled 'Uncertainty', which is somewhat opposite to dogma," they add 
Among the national pavilions, in the Giardini, it stands out, for its simplicity and effectiveness, that of Denmark  It is crossed by a course of water of rainforest origin, which flows through a channel dug into the concrete floor  In one room rains, in another there is a lake surrounded by a chill out that in summer will be one of the coolest places in the Biennial  The enclosure lattice supports infusion plants, which are then served on site  Everything is connected in nature  Hence the title of this work by architects Lundgaard and Trauberg, and artist Reinbothe: Connectivity 
But if there is a dominant theme among the invited countries that is that of wood, building material booming  The U S  exhibits its tradition of wooden structures, with mock-ups, photos and - proof of faith - a new-storey, three-level facade, walkable, attached to the original pavilion  Finland reviews its wooden architecture from World War II to today  And Japan abounds on this issue, but emphasizing sustainability, by displaying the wooden components - some reused on the outside of the pavilion - of a house built in 1954 in Tokyo, renovated, dismantled and now exhibited by pieces in Venice 
Canada addresses in Impostor Cities the issue of loss of identity (after becoming a set of many film productions that use its cities and landscapes and then attribute them to other countries); Switzerland deals with the issue of borders, presenting them not as a barrier but as an opportunity for encounter  Ireland warns in Entanglement about the dangers of the cloud in which we hang our documents, in a noisy installation with Echoes of Mad Max, to remind us that 30% of electricity expenditure will soon go towards cooling computer-filled data centers  Which tells us that the cloud is not etherea and immaterial, but punishes the Earth  Finally, in pandemic times, Germany has left its pavilion empty, except for mirrors on the ground to store the distance and QR codes on the walls that give the information 
The Biennial includes many more ideas than fit in this personal selection  Asked whether, once all these participations were gathered, he already had an answer to the initial question that summoned them, Sarkisdeclared the day before yesterday: "There is not a single source, not a single idea  What this selection tells us is that we must accept diversity and foster dialogue to learn from each other "
Catalonia, the air that is breathed
Olga Subir'os, curator of the Pavilion of Catalonia in the Biennial, goes further and not only wonders how we will live together, but how we will survive together  That is why it has been proposed that their work serve to raise awareness among architects and urban planners about the problem of the low air quality we breathe; of an air stale by polluting gas emissions from vehicles, trapped between buildings by the so-called street canyon effect  These emissions are attributed to seven million dead per year, 400,000 of them in Europe, more than 2,000 in Barcelona  Its installation consists of two long walls  On their outer faces are offered audiovisual with pollution data in Barcelona and the world, respectively  The work of collecting this data - from these tests - has been carried out by the team 300,000 kms  Between the two walls is proposed an immersive experience, with an audible installation due to John Talabot and performed by Maria Arnal, while we are reminded of the dimension of the problem, our vulnerability and what we can do  "We have mapped this problem, providing proof of the dangers involved  It is imperative that all architects know it, act accordingly and transform reality", concludes Subir'os

headline: Software company behind plan for data centers in Hoffman Estates
text:
Microsoft has been named as the previously anonymous company behind a proposal to build a pair of 207,000-square-foot internet data centers in Hoffman Estates  The 53-acre site on Lakewood Avenue north of the Bell Works Chicagoland "Metroburb" initially was billed as future home of the "Hoffman Technology Park," but its developers sold the land to Microsoft last summer  "The data center will be a welcome addition," Hoffman Estates Mayor Bill McLeod said Tuesday  
"They're investing a ton of money " Village officials said the data center is estimated to create 280 construction jobs over the course of the phased project, and 20 permanent jobs per building  The property value is anticipated to be above $200 million when the data center is completed  McLeod said the village has been in talks with Microsoft for about two years, a process that required the signing of a confidentiality agreement  "The board and I have to go out to aggressively seek businesses to bring to Hoffman Estates," he said  McLeod believes the completion of a full interchange at the I-90 tollway and Barrington Road contributed to Microsoft choosing the site, as did the cluster of new commercial buildings near the former AT&T corporate campus  "Trends always change," McLeod said of the loss and replacement of the AT&T headquarters  "You have to be able to pivot " Though many of the details of the data center project were revealed last fall, McLeod believes confirmation of Microsoft involvement at this point makes a difference  "It makes it real," he said  "Microsoft is a real company with real resources " McLeod said Microsoft has committed to making the site attractive and the final look may be somewhat different from renderings made public in the fall  Full details of the project are expected to be reviewed soon by Hoffman Estates' planning and zoning commission  But first, the village board is poised on Monday to recommend a Class 6B tax incentive to the Cook County Board and to allow the clearing of trees on the site  The Class 6B incentive is intended as a way to make Cook County competitive with the nearby collar counties for commercial development  The incentive essentially cut a site property tax assessment in half for 10 years, and then gradually increases it during the 11th and 12th years before returning to normal  The village must also first formally terminate the development agreement it had with the Hoffman Technology Park that included the same initial tax break  If approved, it would become Microsoft second data center project in the Northwest suburbs  In early 2020, the company closed on a $52 2 million purchase of 36 acres in Elk Grove Village new Elk Grove Technology Park
